---
title: "RAG & Document Retrieval"
guide-section: "Architecture"
---

# RAG & Document Retrieval

The system pairs **LangChain agents** with **Vertex AI RAG** tools. LangChain
handles agent orchestration and tool calling; Vertex AI Search provides dense
vector retrieval over the Oregon housing-law corpus; Gemini writes the answer.

## Architecture type

- **Framework** — LangChain 1.1+.
- **LLM integration** — `ChatGoogleGenerativeAI` (langchain-google-genai 4.0+).
- **Agent pattern** — `create_agent()` with custom RAG tools.
- **Retrieval** — dense vector similarity search with metadata filtering, via
  `VertexAISearchRetriever`.

## Agent tools

The agent has three tools, and the LLM decides which to call based on the user's
query and location. One retrieves law; the other two draft a tenant letter.

### Retrieval

[`retrieve_city_state_laws`](../reference/langchain_tools.retrieve_city_state_laws.qmd)
searches documents filtered by state and, when supplied, city. City-scoped
queries also include state-level ("null") documents so the agent sees both layers
of law in one retrieval.

Retrieval quality lives in
[`RagBuilder`](../reference/langchain_tools.RagBuilder.qmd), which configures the
`VertexAISearchRetriever` (extractive segments over answers, suggestion-only
spell correction, retry with backoff) and repairs mojibake in returned passages.
The jurisdiction filter is built by
[`filter_builder`](../reference/langchain_tools.filter_builder.qmd).

### Letter drafting

Two tools let the agent produce a formatted tenant letter instead of inline chat
prose:

- [`get_letter_template`](../reference/langchain_tools.get_letter_template.qmd)
  returns a pre-formatted letter template for the model to fill in.
- [`generate_letter`](../reference/langchain_tools.generate_letter.qmd) emits the
  completed letter as a custom stream chunk so the frontend can render it in a
  separate letter panel rather than inline chat text.

## Agent entry points

The agent graph is defined once in `graph.py` by
[`create_graph`](../reference/graph.create_graph.qmd) and consumed by two entry
points:

::: {#fig-agent-entry-points}
```{mermaid}
graph TB
    GraphDef["graph.py<br/>create_graph()"]

    subgraph WebApp["Web application"]
        ChatMgr["LangChainChatManager<br/>(per session)"]
        ChatMgr --> PerSessionGraph["Graph with location<br/>context baked into<br/>the system prompt"]
    end

    subgraph CloudDeploy["LangGraph dev / Cloud"]
        ManifestRef["langgraph.json<br/>references graph()"]
        ManifestRef --> DeployGraph["Deployment graph<br/>middleware injects<br/>context at runtime"]
    end

    GraphDef --> WebApp
    GraphDef --> CloudDeploy
```

One graph factory, two entry points: the web app and the LangGraph deployment.
:::

- **Web application** —
  [`LangChainChatManager`](../reference/langchain_chat_manager.LangChainChatManager.qmd)
  calls `create_graph()` with a per-session system prompt built by
  [`prepare_system_prompt`](../reference/graph.prepare_system_prompt.qmd), which
  bakes in the user's city/state. It streams response chunks back to Flask.
- **LangGraph dev / Cloud** — `langgraph.json` points at the module-level `graph`
  factory in `graph.py`. Studio middleware injects an editable system prompt at
  runtime, enabling `langgraph dev` for local testing and LangSmith Cloud for
  browser-based evaluation. See the [Evaluation Guide](../evaluation-guide/index.qmd).

## Query pipeline

::: {#fig-query-pipeline}
```{mermaid}
sequenceDiagram
    participant User
    participant Flask as Flask API
    participant Session as Chat Manager
    participant Gemini
    participant RAG as Vertex AI RAG
    participant Corpus as Document Corpus

    User->>Flask: POST /api/query
    Flask->>Session: Attach conversation history
    Flask->>Gemini: Generate response with RAG tool
    Gemini->>RAG: Retrieve relevant documents
    RAG->>Corpus: Query with user context
    Corpus-->>RAG: Return relevant passages
    RAG-->>Gemini: Provide context
    Gemini-->>Flask: Stream response chunks
    Flask-->>User: Stream response
```

The query pipeline, from user request through RAG retrieval to a streamed answer.
:::

1. **Context preparation** — the query is combined with conversation history and
   location context.
2. **RAG retrieval** — Vertex AI RAG searches the corpus for relevant passages.
3. **Response generation** — Gemini answers using the retrieved documents.
4. **Streaming** — the response streams to the client in real time.
5. **Session update** — conversation state is persisted for continuity.

Where the corpus itself comes from is covered in
[Corpus Ingestion](07-corpus-ingestion.qmd).

## Where to go next

- [Streaming Responses](04-streaming.qmd) — the chunk types and stream mechanics.
- [Conversation Management](05-conversation-management.qmd) — multi-turn state.
